mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.1) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0) in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a stagnant federal funds rate indicate unanchored inflation expectations that force markets to price in future rate hikes, depressing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative Rank IC, or S&P 500 returns are positive in > 50% of high-inflation/low-rate observations over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product exceeding 0.5% triggers recession fears that immediately compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside rising real GDP confirms a synchronized economic upturn that reduces default risk and boosts equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The synchronized expansion signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in regime-filtered samples.
